what: The sprites-vs-cadence receipt: which sprite counts N hold the in-situ +1/+1 loop-vs-NMI lockstep. Committed so the receipt is version-controlled evidence and a drift in the durable points is caught, rather than only landing in /tmp at test time. test_sp_cadence_sweep_curve asserts its live measurement against the durable verdicts below.

builds.full: _spr_sweep - scripted two-pad input + AI pathing + size tiers, full-rate reprojection every frame
builds.altframe: _spr_alt - the index halves reproject alternately at 30 Hz under the 60 Hz display (a probe, not the default)

border_points_recorded_not_asserted.note: 48/64/96 on the full-rate build sit near the cliff and are wall-clock-variance-sensitive; the live full grid over N={8,16,24,32,48,64,96,128} lands in /tmp/e2e_screenshots/task2_curve.json each run.

ship.rule: largest N with +1/+1 lockstep AND >=15% modeled headroom

per_sprite_per_band_mc.note: 5595 is the direct _spr_cyc instrument; the integrated-sweep slope /2 gives 5653 (+1.0%), the cross-check. See docs/guides/split_h.md.

per_scanline_overflow.model: 34 slivers / 4 per 32x32 OBJ = 8; the range-32 limit never binds first
